skill.name: temporal-python-testing
skill.description: Test Temporal workflows with pytest, time-skipping, and mocking strategies. Covers unit testing, integration testing, replay testing, and local development setup.
skill.summary: Comprehensive testing strategies for Temporal Python workflows using pytest with time-skipping and activity mocking.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 140 patterns flagged as security concerns, but all are false positives from Markdown documentation containing Python code examples. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are Python code fences in markdown. URLs are localhost development references. Network, filesystem, and cryptographic findings are documentation examples, not executable code. Safe for publication as educational content.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: Markdown documentation includes shell command examples for Docker and debugging (docker-compose, lsof). These are educational examples in code blocks, not executable code within the skill.

content.value_statement: Testing Temporal workflows is complex due to time-dependent operations and distributed execution. This skill provides proven pytest patterns with time-skipping, activity mocking, and replay testing to achieve reliable test coverage.
actual_capabilities: Guide unit testing of Temporal workflows using WorkflowEnvironment with time-skipping for instant test execution
Demonstrate activity mocking strategies to isolate workflow logic from external dependencies
Provide replay testing patterns to validate workflow determinism against production histories
Show local development setup with Docker Compose for Temporal server and pytest integration
Explain signal and query testing patterns for interactive workflow scenarios
Cover CI/CD integration patterns with automated replay testing and coverage tracking
limitations: Does not provide actual Temporal server infrastructure - requires user to set up their own environment
Code examples are Python-specific and do not cover other Temporal SDK languages
Testing patterns assume pytest framework and may require adaptation for other test frameworks
Does not include production deployment guidance beyond testing workflows

use_cases.title: Backend Developer Testing Workflows
use_cases.description: A Python developer needs to test Temporal workflows that include month-long sleep durations. Use this skill to implement time-skipping tests that complete in milliseconds, achieving fast feedback during development.

use_cases.title: QA Engineer Validating Determinism
use_cases.description: A QA engineer must verify workflow changes do not break existing executions. Use this skill to set up replay testing that validates determinism against production workflow histories before deployment.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up CI/CD
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer needs automated testing for Temporal workflows in CI/CD pipelines. Use this skill to integrate pytest with coverage tracking and automated history export for regression testing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Workflow Test Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a pytest test for a Temporal workflow that includes a 30-day sleep. Show me how to use WorkflowEnvironment with time-skipping so the test completes instantly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner learning basic Temporal testing patterns

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a workflow that calls payment and inventory APIs through activities. Show me how to mock these activities in integration tests to test the workflow orchestration logic without calling real external services.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Workflow Determinism
prompt_templates.prompt: I modified my workflow code and need to verify it is backward-compatible with running workflows. Show me how to export production workflow histories and replay them with the new code to validate determinism.

prompt_templates.title: CI/CD Integration for Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs pytest tests for Temporal workflows, including Docker Compose for the Temporal server, coverage reporting with 80% threshold, and automated history export for replay testing.
prompt_templates.scenario: DevOps engineer implementing automated testing pipeline

output_examples.input: Test a workflow with a 7-day approval timeout
output: Workflow test completes in 50ms using time-skipping mode
All approval path branches tested with parameterized inputs
Coverage report shows 92% workflow logic coverage
2 workflows failed - non-deterministic random number usage detected
Recommendation: Replace random module with workflow.random()

best_practices: Use time-skipping WorkflowEnvironment for all unit tests to achieve fast feedback - month-long workflows should test in milliseconds
Mock all external dependencies in integration tests - never call real APIs during test execution to ensure reliability and speed
Run replay tests against production histories before every deployment - this validates determinism and prevents breaking running workflows
anti_patterns: Using datetime.now() or random module directly in workflows - these break determinism and cause replay failures
Writing end-to-end tests with real Temporal server for every test - this is slow and brittle; prefer time-skipping unit tests
Skipping replay testing before deployment - workflow code changes may break existing executions without proper validation

faq.question: Why do my replay tests fail after refactoring workflow code?
faq.answer: Replay tests fail when code changes alter the sequence of workflow decisions. Ensure you use workflow.now() instead of datetime.now() and workflow.random() instead of random module. Avoid changing activity IDs or conditional branching logic that affects which activities are scheduled.

faq.question: How do I test workflows with long sleep durations without waiting?
faq.answer: Use WorkflowEnvironment.start_time_skipping() which automatically advances time when workflows sleep. A 30-day sleep completes instantly in tests. For manual control, use env.sleep(timedelta(hours=1)) to advance time by specific amounts.

faq.question: Should I test activities with ActivityEnvironment or in a Worker?
faq.answer: Use ActivityEnvironment for isolated unit tests of activity logic - it is faster and does not require a Temporal server. Test activities within a Worker when you need to verify workflow-activity integration, retry behavior, or heartbeat handling.

faq.question: How often should I export production workflow histories for replay testing?
faq.answer: Export histories weekly or after major workflow changes. Keep at least 100 representative histories covering different execution paths. Automate export with a scheduled job that queries recent completed workflows and saves histories to your test fixtures directory.

faq.question: What coverage target should I aim for with Temporal workflows?
faq.answer: Target 80% or higher coverage for workflow logic and critical activities. Use pytest-cov with --cov-fail-under=80 to enforce the threshold in CI. Focus on testing all branching paths, error scenarios, and retry behavior rather than achieving 100% line coverage.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All three AI assistants can help implement the testing patterns, write pytest tests, set up Docker Compose configurations, and integrate replay testing into CI/CD pipelines.
